channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump touts 'no-tax-on-tips' plan in Nevada victory lap
item.description: When Nevada flipped red in November, Trump became the first GOP presidential candidate to win the key swing state since President George W. Bush in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republican urges Trump to spare some migrants under deportation plan
item.description: Salazar, a daughter of Cuban exiles, represents Florida's 27th Congressional District which is home to a number of Venezuelans and Cubans.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: The U.S. Air Force has removed training courses for service members that included historical videos of its storied Black Tuskegee Airmen and Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — female World War II pilots.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli Columbia professor wants Trump to block certain institutions from receiving federal funding
item.description: Columbia associate professor activist Shai Davidai is calling on Trump to stop private institutions from receiving federal funds if they hire professors who support terror groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires 17 government watchdogs at various federal agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump fired more than a dozen government watchdogs late Friday in a move that appears to have violated federal law requiring him to notify Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Loyalty tests and MAGA checks: Inside the Trump White House's intense screening of job-seekers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Job-seekers hoping to join the new Trump administration are facing a series of intense loyalty tests, with White House screening teams fanning out to government agencies to check for “Make America Great Again” bona fides and carefully parsing applicants' politics and social media p
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 5 biggest moves on immigration so far
item.description: President Trump released a flurry of immigration actions this week, signing a series of orders targeting the border and ramping up enforcement. While Trump has long pledged to address illegal immigration, many of his actions targeted longstanding legal pathways.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Pete Hegseth confirmed to lead Pentagon after VP Vance casts tie-breaking vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday night on the confirmation of Trump's pick to lead the Department of Defense, Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: State Supreme Court weighs what to do about ‘dysfunctional’ Minnesota House
item.description: The high court heard oral arguments Thursday over the temporary control of the Minnesota House. “This is a highly unusual situation we find ourselves in,” Justice Anne McKeig said during early questioning of attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden endures painful but active last day of presidency
item.description: When President Biden walked through the Capitol on Monday with mere minutes left in his presidency, a reporter asked how he was feeling.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
